info.title: Seed Stocks Service - Continuous Analysis Pipeline
info.description: Pre-computed stock recommendations with volume intelligence. OpenAPI groups: **Settings (UI)** = editable config; **Observability** = metrics and learning transparency.

get.description:
Check whether a symbol has an open tracked position and its current rank.

Returns:
  - **position_status**: ``open_position`` | ``ranked`` | ``in_universe``
  - **open_position**: True if there is an active tracked position
  - **can_add**: True if no active position exists (eligible for new entry)
  - **rank / score**: from the current ranked_stocks list (None if unranked)

Self-learning: performance aggregated by score bin (0_30, 30_60, 60_80, 80_100).
Returns avg_return_pct, count, success_rate_pct per bin per trade_type per horizon.

schema.description: Days of data when no date range given

get.description:
Enhanced performance analysis with flexible grouping and filtering.

- **group_by=score_bin**: Performance by score bin
- **group_by=source_arm**: Performance by ARM (Chartink query strategy)
- **group_by=outcome_type**: Performance by outcome horizon
- **horizon_type=time**: Only time-based outcomes
- **horizon_type=event**: Only event-based outcomes

schema.description: Group results by: score_bin | source_arm | outcome_type

schema.title: Response Get Regime Scoring Observability V2 Observability Regime Scoring Get

get.description:
Get top stock recommendations for a trade type.

**Cold start**: When no pre-computed rankings exist (e.g. first deploy, empty universe),
the API fetches symbols from the query service (ARMs), builds universe, runs scoring
(using last-close/candle data when market is closed), then returns recommendations.
If the query returns no symbols, empty list is returned (valid scenario).

**Fast path**: < 50ms when rankings exist (reads from pre-computed ranked_stocks).

**Trade Types**:
- `intraday_buy`: Breakout/momentum (hold until EOD)
- `intraday_sell`: Mean reversion/shorting (hold until EOD)
- `swing_buy`: Pullback in uptrend (2-10 days)
- `short_buy`: Short-term buy trades (1-5 days)
- `long_term`: Long-term trends (30-120 days)

**Response includes**:
- Entry price, stop loss, targets
- Risk/reward ratio
- Entry timing windows (for intraday)
- Reasoning and signals
- Technical snapshot

parameters.name: trade_type

schema.description: Trade type: intraday_buy, intraday_sell, swing_buy, short_buy, long_term
schema.title: Trade Type

parameters.description: Trade type: intraday_buy, intraday_sell, swing_buy, short_buy, long_term

parameters.name: min_score

schema.description: Score floor. Omit to use system setting (api_limits.recommendations_min_score_default). 0 = no floor.
schema.title: Min Score

parameters.description: Score floor. Omit to use system setting (api_limits.recommendations_min_score_default). 0 = no floor.

Use ``mover_type=all`` (default) to get all three lists in one response. Kite batch quotes when market open.

**scenario** (or category): `all` | `paper_trade` | `learning`.

get.description:
Recent ARM execution log, newest first.
field_info:
  executed_at       When execution started
  duration_ms       How long the Chartink call took
  records_returned  Stocks returned by the ARM query
  admitted_count    Stocks that passed gatekeeper into universe (may be null)
  status            success|failed|empty
  market_regime     Indian market regime at time of execution
  pipeline_run_id   Groups all ARMs executed in a single pipeline cycle

schema.title: Response Execution Timeline Api V2 Arms Observability Execution Timeline Get

schema.title: Response Recent Runs Api V2 Arms Observability Recent Runs Get

get.description:
Per-ARM aggregated utilization for the last N days.
Shows: how often each ARM ran, how many stocks it found, and success rate.
Useful for spotting ARMs that consistently return 0 stocks or are failing.

schema.title: Response Utilization Api V2 Arms Observability Utilization Get

get.description:
Self-learning state per ARM.
Combines Thompson Sampling weights (from learning service) with position outcomes
(from DB) to show how each ARM is performing and evolving.

field_info:
  thompson_weight     Current selection probability (normalised). Higher = more likely to be selected.
  alpha / beta        Beta distribution params. alpha = weighted successes; beta = weighted failures.
  observations        alpha+beta = total learning observations (not raw trades).
  win_rate_pct        % of closed positions with return > 0.
  avg_return_pct      Average return across closed positions.
  is_probation        ARM is under re-evaluation (lower prior weight seeded).
  regime_weights      Per-regime thompson weights if available.

schema.title: Response Learning Observability Api V2 Arms Observability Learning Get

get.description:

get.description:
**Purpose:** Comprehensive ARM performance analytics with market context awareness

**When to use:** Monitor ARM performance across different market conditions, identify patterns and trends in ARM behavior

**How to use:** Specify scenario or specific ARM names. Use analysis_days to control lookback period. Enable include_market_context for regime-specific metrics

**Purpose:** Identify ARMs that need optimization attention based on performance analysis

**When to use:** Regular performance reviews, troubleshooting underperforming strategies, planning optimization work

**How to use:** Filter by scenario and severity. Results ordered by optimization priority

**Purpose:** ARM performance across different market conditions in matrix/heatmap format

**When to use:** Understand which ARMs work best in which market conditions, identify market adaptability gaps

**How to use:** Specify ARM names and market dimensions. Returns performance data suitable for heatmap visualization

**Purpose:** Learning convergence analysis and error timeline for ARM Thompson sampling

**When to use:** Diagnose learning issues, understand convergence patterns, identify ARMs with unstable learning

**How to use:** Specify ARM names and timeline period. Returns convergence metrics and learning progression

**Purpose:** Performance improvement comparison and tracking over time

**When to use:** Measure optimization impact, track ARM performance improvements, validate optimization efforts

**How to use:** Specify comparison periods and ARM names. Returns improvement metrics with statistical significance

**Purpose:** Overall ARM ecosystem health metrics and system-wide performance indicators

**When to use:** System health monitoring, executive dashboards, overall performance assessment

**How to use:** No parameters needed. Returns comprehensive ecosystem health score and metrics

**Purpose:** ARM variant A/B testing performance and comparison results

**When to use:** Monitor ARM variant testing, evaluate optimization results, make promotion decisions

**How to use:** Provide variant ID from optimization engine. Returns A/B testing results and recommendation

**Purpose:** In-process rolling latency (p50/p95/p99) per HTTP route for scaling decisions.

**When to use:** Suspect slow APIs or before load tests; compare with /performance/external.

**How to use:** GET; sorted by p95 descending. Populated by RequestIDMiddleware after traffic.

**Purpose:** Latency of outbound Chartink/Kite/Yahoo calls (@track_latency).

**When to use:** API is slow but /performance shows low — check here for dependency bottlenecks.

**How to use:** GET; keys like kite.get_batch_quotes, chartink.execute_query.

**Purpose:** Inventory of every mounted route (method, path, tags) for clients and audits.

**When to use:** Generating client SDKs or verifying a path exists after deploy.

**How to use:** GET; scan routes[] for method+path.

get.summary: Get rate limiting status for specific service
get.description:
**Purpose:** Monitor rate limiting for a specific external service (kite, yahoo, chartink)

**When to use:** Debug rate limiting issues for specific services, monitor service-specific usage

**How to use:** Specify service name to get detailed rate limiting status

get.summary: Get configured rate limits for all services
get.description:
**Purpose:** View the configured rate limits and policies for all external services

**When to use:** Understanding service limits, configuration review, capacity planning

**How to use:** Call to see current rate limit configuration

post.summary: Reset rate limiting state for a service
post.description:
**Purpose:** Reset rate limiting state for a service that may be stuck or blocked

**When to use:** Service appears to be stuck in blocked state, emergency rate limit reset

**How to use:** Specify service name to reset its rate limiting state

post.summary: Initialize or reinitialize rate limiting system
post.description:
**Purpose:** Initialize rate limiting system with optional Redis coordination

**When to use:** System startup, configuration changes, switching coordination modes

**How to use:** Call to initialize or reinitialize the rate limiting system

get.description:
**Purpose:** Generate detailed report of rate limiting usage patterns and efficiency

**When to use:** Performance analysis, capacity planning, optimization review

**How to use:** Call to get comprehensive usage statistics and recommendations

post.description:
**Purpose:** Gracefully shutdown the rate limiting system

**When to use:** Application shutdown, maintenance, resource cleanup

**How to use:** Call during application shutdown to clean up resources

get.summary: Get Yahoo Finance free tier usage statistics
get.description:
**Purpose:** Monitor Yahoo Finance API usage to stay within free tier limits

**When to use:** Monitor API quota usage, troubleshoot Yahoo rate limiting issues

**How to use:** Call regularly to track Yahoo Finance API consumption and optimize usage

get.summary: Check if Yahoo quota is available for requests
get.description:
**Purpose:** Check if Yahoo Finance API quota is available before making requests

**When to use:** Before critical Yahoo requests, in request planning, quota management

**How to use:** Call to verify quota availability before making Yahoo API requests

post.description:
**Purpose:** Reset the daily request counter for Yahoo Finance (typically done automatically)

**When to use:** Administrative reset, troubleshooting, daily maintenance

**How to use:** POST to reset the daily counter - use carefully

**Purpose:** Clear cached Yahoo Finance data to force fresh requests

**When to use:** Troubleshooting stale data, testing, cache management

**How to use:** POST to clear all cached Yahoo Finance data

get.summary: Get Yahoo free tier optimization report
get.description:
**Purpose:** Get comprehensive report on Yahoo Finance usage optimization

**When to use:** Performance analysis, optimization review, usage planning

**How to use:** Call to get detailed analysis of Yahoo Finance usage patterns and recommendations
Get current ARM filter configuration and status
Get ARM performance analysis report

schema.title: Response Get Arm Performance Report Api Functional Arms Performance Report Get

post.description: Update ARM filter strictness level
Enable emergency ARM filtering (crisis mode - only top performers)
Disable emergency ARM filtering (back to balanced mode)

get.description: Get top performing ARMs by expected return

schema.title: Response Get Top Performing Arms Api Functional Arms Top Performers Get

get.description: Get currently blocked ARMs with reasons

schema.title: Response Get Blocked Arms Api Functional Arms Blocked Arms Get

get.description:
Get learning system alert status summary

Returns current alert counts and system health status.
Use this for dashboard health indicators.

Get all current learning system alerts

Returns detailed list of active alerts with full context.
Use this for detailed alert investigation.

schema.title: Response Get Current Learning Alerts Api Learning Alerts Alerts Get

post.description:
Trigger immediate learning system health check

Forces a fresh check of all learning components.
Returns updated alert summary.

get.description:
Get recent learning alert history

Returns chronological list of recent alerts for trend analysis.

Get current ARM selection status and context

Shows which ARMs are currently active/blocked and why.
Useful for understanding market context impact.

Get detailed learning pipeline health metrics

Provides deep dive into learning system component health.
Get current return calculation system status

schema.title: Response Get Return Calculation Status Api Return Calculations Status Get

post.description:
**Purpose:** Create new ARM variant for manual optimization testing

**When to use:** Manual ARM optimization experiments, parameter testing

**How to use:** POST JSON with parent ARM name, modifications, and test conditions

schema.title: Response Create Arm Variant Api V2 Settings Arm Optimization Variants Post

put.description:
**Purpose:** Promote successful ARM variant to production

**When to use:** After A/B testing confirms variant outperforms parent

**How to use:** PUT to promote variant. Optionally replace parent ARM entirely

schema.title: Response Promote Arm Variant Api V2 Settings Arm Optimization Variants  Variant Id  Promote Put

post.description:
**Purpose:** Trigger automatic optimization for underperforming ARMs

**When to use:** Scheduled optimization runs or manual trigger for optimization

**How to use:** POST with optional scenario filter. Creates variants for underperformers

schema.title: Response Trigger Auto Optimization Api V2 Settings Arm Optimization Auto Optimize Post

delete.description:
**Purpose:** Cleanup old deprecated ARM variants

**When to use:** Periodic maintenance to remove old unused variants

**How to use:** DELETE request removes deprecated variants older than specified days

schema.title: Response Cleanup Deprecated Variants Api V2 Settings Arm Optimization Variants Cleanup Delete

get.description:
**Purpose:** Raw ARM optimization configuration including thresholds, automation settings, and alerts

**When to use:** Programmatic read of ARM optimization settings for API integration

**How to use:** GET request returns full configuration. Pair with PUT for updates

schema.title: Response Get Arm Optimization Config Api V2 Settings Arm Optimization Get

put.description:
**Purpose:** Update ARM optimization settings with validation

**When to use:** Apply configuration changes from UI or API clients

**How to use:** PUT JSON with nested keys matching ArmOptimizationConfig structure. Partial updates supported

schema.title: Response Update Arm Optimization Config Api V2 Settings Arm Optimization Put

get.description:
**Purpose:** JSON Schema for ARM optimization configuration

**When to use:** Generate UI forms or validate configuration programmatically

**How to use:** GET request returns JSON Schema suitable for form generation

schema.title: Response Get Arm Optimization Schema Api V2 Settings Arm Optimization Schema Get

get.description:
**Purpose:** UI-friendly ARM optimization configuration with field descriptions and grouping

**When to use:** Load ARM optimization settings page in UI

**How to use:** GET request returns structured configuration with field explanations and commonly changed fields highlighted

schema.title: Response Get Arm Optimization Form Api V2 Settings Arm Optimization Form Get

tags.name: Settings (UI)
tags.description: Runtime configuration allowed from the System Settings UI: trading limits, system API limits, staleness thresholds, alerts, and position-opener cutoffs. Prefer GET …/form for field explanations, then PUT with partial JSON patches.

tags.name: Observability
tags.description: Read-only transparency: pipeline health, ARM runs, learning metrics, latency, registry stats, and candidate coverage. Use these to monitor and tune the system; they do not replace Settings for editable config.

tags.name: Registry — ARMs
tags.description: DB-backed ARM registry: list, verify query, create/update.

tags.name: Candidates
tags.description: Candidate stocks from ARM universe + fundamentals history.

tags.name: Backtesting
tags.description: Historical strategy testing and performance analysis.

tags.name: Execution Quality
tags.description: Execution analysis: slippage, timing, liquidity conditions.

tags.name: Learning Governance
tags.description: Thompson sampling oversight and learning system health.

tags.name: Performance Attribution
tags.description: Multi-dimensional return analysis by ARM, sector, regime.

tags.name: Regime Analysis
tags.description: Advanced market regime detection and trading implications.

tags.name: Advanced Monitoring
tags.description: Comprehensive system monitoring, alerting, and health checks.

tags.name: Data Quality
tags.description: Data quality monitoring, validation, and governance framework.

tags.name: System Optimization
tags.description: CPU optimization, parallel processing, and system performance tuning.

tags.name: Rate Limiting
tags.description: External API rate limiting monitoring and management.

tags.name: Yahoo Free Tier
tags.description: Yahoo Finance free tier quota monitoring and optimization.
